title: Oscar and the Case of the Shrinking Cookies
synopsis: When Oscar's cookies keep mysteriously shrinking, he teams up with his friend Maya to solve the case using science and observation. Their investigation leads them to discover the real culprit behind the disappearing treats.

pages.text: Oscar loved to bake cookies with his mom. Today they made chocolate chip cookies. They smelled so good! "Can I have one now?" Oscar asked. "After lunch," Mom said with a smile. Oscar put three big cookies on his special blue plate. He set them on the kitchen counter. "I'll eat them after my sandwich," he said to himself.

illustration.description: A cheerful kitchen scene with Oscar, a 6-year-old boy with curly brown hair and bright green eyes, standing on a step stool next to his mom, a woman with long black hair in a ponytail. They're both smiling as they look at freshly baked chocolate chip cookies cooling on a rack. The kitchen has warm yellow walls, colorful dish towels, and sunlight streaming through a window.
illustration.alt: Oscar and his mom baking cookies in a bright, cozy kitchen

pages.text: After lunch, Oscar ran to get his cookies. But something was wrong! The cookies looked smaller. Much smaller! "That's weird," Oscar said. He picked up one cookie. It was only half the size it was before. "Mom!" he called. "My cookies shrunk!" Mom came to look. "Hmm, that is strange," she said. "Maybe you remember them being bigger than they were."

illustration.description: Oscar stands in front of the kitchen counter with a confused and surprised expression, holding a tiny cookie between his thumb and finger. His big green eyes are wide with wonder. The blue plate sits on the counter with two other small cookies. The lighting emphasizes the mysterious nature of the scene with dramatic shadows.
illustration.alt: Oscar looking puzzled while holding a mysteriously small cookie

pages.text: But Oscar knew his cookies had been big. Really big! He was good at noticing things. His teacher Mrs. Rodriguez always said he had detective eyes. "I need to solve this mystery," Oscar said. He got his notebook and pencil. At the top of the page, he wrote: "The Case of the Shrinking Cookies." Then he drew a picture of the tiny cookies. This was going to be his first real mystery!

illustration.description: Oscar sits at a small wooden table with intense concentration, writing in a red notebook. His pencil is poised thoughtfully near his mouth as he thinks. Around him are scattered crayons and his detective supplies. The scene has warm, cozy lighting that shows his determination and excitement about solving the mystery.
illustration.alt: Oscar writing in his detective notebook, looking determined to solve the mystery

pages.text: The next morning, Oscar had a plan. He would make new cookies and watch them carefully. His friend Maya came over to help. Maya had short black hair and wore purple glasses. She was really good at science. "We need to be scientific about this," Maya said. "Let's measure everything!" They got Mom's ruler. The new cookies were three inches wide. Oscar wrote this down in his notebook.

illustration.description: Oscar and Maya in the kitchen, both leaning over the counter with a ruler measuring fresh cookies. Maya is a girl with short black hair, purple glasses, and a bright smile. She's wearing a yellow t-shirt. Both children look focused and excited about their scientific investigation. Cookie sheets and measuring tools are scattered around them.
illustration.alt: Oscar and Maya measuring cookies with a ruler as part of their investigation

pages.text: Oscar and Maya put the measured cookies on the blue plate. They hid behind the kitchen door to watch. And wait. And wait some more. "This is boring," Maya whispered. "Shh!" Oscar whispered back. "Good detectives are patient." They waited for one whole hour. Nothing happened. The cookies stayed the same size. "Maybe the cookie thief only comes when no one is watching," Oscar said.

illustration.description: Oscar and Maya peeking around a white kitchen door, both with wide curious eyes and finger to their lips in a 'shh' gesture. Oscar has his detective notebook clutched in his free hand. The scene shows them in a spy-like pose with exaggerated, playful expressions. The kitchen counter with the blue plate of cookies is visible in the background.
illustration.alt: Oscar and Maya secretly watching their cookies from behind the kitchen door

pages.text: "I have an idea!" Maya said. "Let's set a trap!" They sprinkled flour all around the plate. "If someone touches the cookies, they'll leave footprints," Maya explained. Oscar thought this was very smart. They also set up Maya's tablet to record a video. "Now we'll catch the cookie thief for sure!" Oscar said. They put the cookies out and went to play in the backyard. This time, they would have proof!

illustration.description: A bird's eye view of the kitchen counter showing the blue plate surrounded by a ring of white flour. Maya's tablet is propped up nearby, recording. The scene has a detective-story feel with dramatic lighting. Oscar and Maya's hands are visible as they carefully sprinkle the flour, their faces showing concentration and excitement about their clever plan.
illustration.alt: Oscar and Maya setting up their flour trap around the plate of cookies

pages.text: After an hour of playing, Oscar and Maya rushed back inside. The flour was everywhere! But the footprints were very small. Too small for a person. "Look!" Maya pointed at the tablet screen. "We caught the thief!" On the video, they saw a tiny gray mouse. It was nibbling the edges of each cookie, making them smaller and smaller. "So that's why they were shrinking!" Oscar laughed.

illustration.description: Oscar and Maya huddled around the tablet screen, both with delighted expressions and pointing at the video. On the screen, a cute gray mouse with big black eyes can be seen nibbling a cookie. The kitchen counter shows the aftermath with flour scattered about and tiny mouse footprints. The lighting is bright and cheerful, showing their joy at solving the mystery.
illustration.alt: Oscar and Maya discovering the mouse on the tablet video, looking excited about solving the mystery

pages.text: "The mouse was so small, it couldn't eat whole cookies," Maya said. "So it just ate little bits around the edges!" Oscar wrote this down in his detective notebook. "Mystery solved!" he said proudly. Just then, they saw the little gray mouse peek out from behind the toaster. It looked hungry. "Don't worry, little guy," Oscar said softly. "We're not mad at you. You were just hungry."

illustration.description: A sweet scene showing the tiny gray mouse peeking out from behind a silver toaster, with big innocent black eyes and whiskers twitching. Oscar and Maya are in the background, both smiling kindly at the mouse. The mouse looks adorable and slightly nervous. The scene has warm, gentle lighting that emphasizes the friendly resolution.
illustration.alt: The little gray mouse peeking out from behind the toaster while Oscar and Maya watch kindly

pages.text: Oscar and Maya asked Mom to help them make a tiny house for the mouse. They put it in the garden with some small pieces of cookies inside. "Now you have your own home and your own cookies," Oscar told the mouse. The mouse seemed very happy. Oscar closed his detective notebook. He had solved his first mystery! "What should we investigate next?" Maya asked. Oscar grinned. "I don't know, but I can't wait to find out!"

illustration.description: A beautiful garden scene showing Oscar and Maya kneeling beside a small wooden mouse house they've built. The gray mouse is nearby, happily eating cookie crumbs. The garden has colorful flowers, green grass, and warm sunlight. All three characters look content and happy. Oscar is holding his red detective notebook with a satisfied smile.
illustration.alt: Oscar and Maya in the garden with the mouse's new home, all looking happy with the solution

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What did Oscar notice was wrong with his cookies?
questions.explanation: Oscar noticed that his cookies looked much smaller than when he first put them on the plate.

questions.question: How did Oscar and Maya try to catch the cookie thief?
choices: They set up flour and a video camera
questions.explanation: Oscar and Maya sprinkled flour around the cookies to catch footprints and used Maya's tablet to record video of what was happening.

questions.question: Who was really taking the cookies?
questions.explanation: The video showed that a small gray mouse was nibbling the edges of the cookies, making them appear to shrink.

questions.question: How did Oscar and Maya help the mouse at the end?
choices: They made it a house with cookies
They took it to the park
questions.explanation: Oscar and Maya built a tiny house for the mouse in the garden and gave it some cookie pieces so it would have its own home and food.

questions.question: What did Oscar use to solve the mystery?
questions.explanation: Oscar used careful observation, scientific methods like measuring and recording, and teamwork with Maya to solve the mystery.

pages.title: Fill in the Detective Story
pages.instructions: Help complete Oscar's detective report by choosing the right words!
pages.text: Oscar found a big ___1___ in his kitchen. His cookies were getting ___2___ every day! He used his ___3___ to write down clues. Maya helped him set up a ___4___ to catch the thief. They discovered that a tiny ___5___ was nibbling the cookies!

pages.title: Oscar's Investigation Steps
pages.instructions: Put Oscar's detective work in the right order by filling in what happened!
pages.text: First, Oscar ___1___ his cookies were shrinking. Next, he wrote in his ___2___ to keep track of clues. Then, Maya helped him ___3___ the cookies to be scientific. They set up a ___4___ with flour and a camera. Finally, they ___5___ the mouse and gave it a new home!
